description: Secure path handling for Rust — prevents directory traversal, symlink escapes, and Windows 8.3 short-name attacks with compile-time guarantees via StrictPath and VirtualPath types
rules: Always validate untrusted paths via strict_join() or virtual_join() before any filesystem I/O
Use StrictPath for 90% of cases (detect and reject escape attempts); use VirtualPath only for containment scenarios (multi-tenant isolation, sandboxes)
Use interop_path() ONLY when a third-party crate requires AsRef<Path>; NEVER expose it to end-users
Use strictpath_display() for system/admin output; virtualpath_display() for user-facing output — never leak real host paths
NEVER validate hardcoded string literals — always validate actual untrusted input from HTTP requests, CLI args, config files, archives, or LLM output
Prefer PathBoundary/VirtualRoot policy types for reuse and performance; use sugar constructors (with_boundary/with_root) only for one-off operations
